# Collectrequirementsprocess

**Project:** ADPA – Advanced Document Processing & Automation Framework  
**Version:** 3.2.0  
**Last Updated:** June 2025

---

## 1. Purpose

This document describes the Collect Requirements process for the ADPA (Advanced Document Processing & Automation Framework) project. It defines the systematic, standards-aligned approach for identifying, capturing, documenting, and validating requirements from all relevant stakeholders to ensure the framework delivers maximum business value, regulatory compliance, and seamless enterprise integration.

---

## 2. Process Objectives

- **Capture Complete & Accurate Requirements:** Gather functional, non-functional, and regulatory requirements from stakeholders across business analysis, project management, and data management domains.
- **Ensure Standards Compliance:** Align requirements with industry frameworks (BABOK v3, PMBOK 7th Edition, DMBOK 2.0) and regulatory mandates (GDPR, SOX, PCI DSS, Basel III, etc.).
- **Support Modular, Scalable Design:** Facilitate requirements gathering that supports ADPA’s modular, API-first, and microservices architecture.
- **Enable AI-Driven Automation:** Identify requirements for intelligent document generation, AI provider integration, and workflow automation.
- **Promote Usability & Integration:** Ensure requirements address CLI, REST API, web interface, and third-party integration (Confluence, SharePoint, Adobe, etc.) needs.

---

## 3. Inputs

- **Business Case & Project Charter**
- **Stakeholder Register & Analysis**
- **Industry Standards:** BABOK v3, PMBOK 7, DMBOK 2.0
- **Existing Documentation:** User stories, templates, usage guides, regulatory matrices
- **Technical Architecture:** Solution overview, integration points, and constraints
- **Regulatory & Security Requirements**
- **Enterprise IT Policies**

---

## 4. Tools & Techniques

- **Workshops & Interviews:** Facilitate sessions with business analysts, project managers, compliance, IT, and end users.
- **Surveys & Questionnaires:** Collect broad input on desired features, pain points, and priorities.
- **Document Analysis:** Review existing templates, process documents, and prior deliverables.
- **Prototyping:** Use CLI, web admin UI, and API endpoints to validate requirements and gather feedback.
- **AI-Assisted Elicitation:** Leverage ADPA’s own AI-powered context management and generation tools to draft initial requirements and gap analyses.
- **Standards Mapping:** Systematically map requirements to BABOK v3, PMBOK 7, and DMBOK 2.0 knowledge areas.
- **Integration Assessments:** Analyze requirements for external system integration (Atlassian, Microsoft, Adobe, etc.).
- **Regulatory Traceability:** Confirm traceability from requirements to compliance controls (GDPR, SOX, etc.).

---

## 5. Key Activities & Steps

### 5.1 Initiate Requirements Collection

- Appoint a Lead Business Analyst (LBA) for overall coordination.
- Identify and invite key stakeholder groups (business, IT, compliance, end users, enterprise architects).
- Define requirements categories: functional, non-functional, compliance, integration, scalability, security.

### 5.2 Elicit Requirements

- Conduct workshops and interviews for each major ADPA capability (document generation, AI integration, workflow automation, etc.).
- Use existing standards (BABOK v3, PMBOK 7, DMBOK 2.0) as checklists for completeness.
- Capture requirements for all supported interfaces: CLI, REST API, web admin portal.

### 5.3 Document Requirements

- Record requirements in a centralized repository (e.g., Confluence, SharePoint, or ADPA’s own document generator).
- Use structured templates aligned to BABOK/PMBOK best practices.
- Ensure traceability: each requirement must link to a stakeholder, business objective, or compliance need.

### 5.4 Analyze & Prioritize

- Classify requirements by priority (Must, Should, Could, Won’t).
- Validate requirements against enterprise constraints (security, scalability, technology stack).
- Identify and resolve conflicts or overlaps.

### 5.5 Validate & Approve

- Review requirements with stakeholders using ADPA’s document output or live review sessions.
- Incorporate feedback and obtain formal sign-off.
- Update requirements repository with version control and change management.

---

## 6. Unique Considerations for ADPA

- **AI Provider Flexibility:** Capture requirements for multi-provider AI orchestration (OpenAI, Google AI, Azure, GitHub Copilot, Ollama), including failover, configuration, and dynamic switching (see implementation guide for interactive provider menu).
- **Standards Compliance Automation:** Document requirements for automatic mapping and validation of project artifacts against BABOK, PMBOK, and DMBOK standards.
- **Enterprise Integrations:** Identify requirements for seamless integration with Confluence, SharePoint, Adobe Document Services, enterprise SSO, and version control systems.
- **Security & Compliance:** Ensure requirements address all regulatory mandates and enterprise-grade authentication, authorization, and audit trail needs.
- **Scalability & Modularity:** Capture requirements supporting microservices, horizontal scaling, and modular deployment (CLI, API, Web UI, Docker/K8s).
- **Collaboration & Workflow:** Gather requirements for multi-user collaboration, approval workflows, real-time editing, and audit trails (see collaboration roadmap).
- **Template-Driven Generation:** Specify requirements for customizable, standards-aligned document templates and output formats (Markdown, PDF, JSON, etc.).

---

## 7. Outputs

- **Requirements Documentation** (standards-compliant, version-controlled)
- **Requirements Traceability Matrix** (mapping requirements to standards, business objectives, and compliance controls)
- **Validated Templates** (BABOK v3, PMBOK 7, DMBOK 2.0)
- **Stakeholder Approval Records**
- **Change Log & Version History**

---

## 8. Roles & Responsibilities

| Role                    | Responsibilities                                              |
|-------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| Lead Business Analyst   | Facilitate process, ensure standards compliance              |
| Project Manager         | Oversee schedule, resource alignment, escalation             |
| Solution Architect      | Validate technical feasibility, integration points           |
| Security Lead           | Ensure regulatory, security, and compliance alignment        |
| AI Integration Engineer | Capture and validate AI provider and automation requirements |
| Stakeholders            | Provide input, review, and approve requirements              |

---

## 9. Best Practices

- **Leverage ADPA’s own automation tools** to accelerate requirements documentation and validation.
- **Maintain rigorous version control** on requirements documentation and templates.
- **Engage all integration partners early** to ensure interoperability and data flow requirements are fully captured.
- **Map every requirement to a business value or compliance objective** to avoid scope creep.
- **Continuously review for standards alignment** as industry frameworks evolve.

---

## 10. Practical Guidance

- Use ADPA CLI (`adpa generate --key requirements-specification`) or REST API (`POST /api/v1/generate`) to produce standards-aligned requirements documentation automatically.
- Employ the admin web interface for collaborative requirements workshops and versioned documentation.
- Validate requirements by generating sample output documents (e.g., project charters, compliance matrices) and reviewing with business/technical stakeholders.
- Integrate with Confluence/SharePoint for centralized, enterprise-grade requirements management.
- For AI provider integration, utilize the interactive selection menu to validate system compatibility and provider-specific requirements.

---

- **PMBOK 7th Ed.**: Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMI)
